title: Locking Support on DB2
text: Locking Support on DB2 Full Uniface locking support (optimistic, cautious, and paranoid) is available. To ensure a lock is set when modifying a record, the default locking connector option must be set to cautious . If it is not set, or is set to optimistic , the lock ProcScript statement does not lock the record. Minimize Locking Conflicts The default DB2 response to a lock is to wait until the lock is removed. If, while attempting to read a record, the connector finds that the record is locked, it waits for the lock to be removed. This can create locking conflicts. The following connector options can be used to help minimize and manage locking conflicts in multi-user applications: default locking lock isolation update isolation To help minimize locking conflicts in multi-user applications, you can set the the locking type and isolation level using the and connector options. The most appropriate Uniface locking methods are optimistic and cautious . Optimistic locking should be used with the cursor stability isolation level. This is the connector default. Cautious locking should be used with the read stability or repeatable read isolation levels. If you are using cautious locking, it can be useful to set a lock timeout using the maxlockwait connector option. Paranoid locking can only be set using the read /lock ProcScript statement. CAUTION: Paranoid locking can cause significant lock contention problems. For details about isolation level, consult your DB2 documentation. Compatiblity Prior to patched P220 on Uniface 9.3 and R111 on Uniface 9.4, the connector only locked the last record modified; the locks on any other records that had been modified without being committed were removed. To achieve the same behavior you need to set the update isolation option to cursor stability . Related Links Locking Strategies Locking means protecting one or more rows from being overwritten by other users while you are working with those rows. Working with rows can be defined as either the simple act of reading those rows from the database, or the act of modifying those rows after retrieving them and before storing, or the act of storing those modified rows. The state that initiates locking depends on the type of locking which has been applied or defined. default locking Set the default locking strategy employed by Uniface. lock isolation Set the isolation level for the database transactions of the connector. update isolation Specify an isolation level to be used specifically by the lock ProcScript statement. read Fetch an occurrence from the hitlist. lock Lock the current occurrence in the database.
